_meta.source: Official BSEB Class 10 syllabus PDFs (Science, Social Science, Mathematics) cross-checked against the original secondary-source compilation
_meta.verified_against: Official BSEB syllabus PDFs (Science.pdf, Social Science.pdf, Maths.pdf) provided 2026-07-21; getmyuni, kollegeapply, careers360, shiksha used only for fields the official PDFs did not cover
_meta.accuracy_note: Subject-wise theory marks (Science: Physics/Chemistry/Biology, Social Science: History/Geography/Political Science/Economics/Disaster Management) and the Mathematics unit split are now OFFICIAL, confirmed against BSEB syllabus PDFs. Chapter-level marks within each subject, the Science unit-wise weightage table (bseb_official_units), and Hindi/English/Sanskrit section breakdowns remain APPROXIMATE — not officially published, unchanged from the prior secondary-source estimate.
verified_fields: Social Science subject-wise split (History 20 / Geography 20 / Political Science 18 / Economics 16 / Disaster Management 6)

general_rules.note_english: English marks are generally excluded from division calculation
general_rules.omr_sheet: Used for Section A objective questions

section_a.name: Section A — Objective MCQ (OMR-based)
section_a.important_rule: If student answers more than 50, only the FIRST 50 answers are evaluated

section_a.name: Section A — Objective MCQ (OMR-based)
section_a.important_rule: If student attempts more than 40, only the FIRST 40 are evaluated

section_d.name: Section D — Internal Assessment (school-based, not in board exam hall)

type2_theory_plus_practical.note_theory_passing: Minimum 33 marks out of 80 required in theory (for Science and Social Science)

chapters: Some Applications of Trigonometry — Heights and Distances (Ch 9)

mathematics.key_focus: Algebra + Trigonometry + Geometry = 60 marks combined — highest priority

chapters.title: Light: Reflection and Refraction
topics: Refraction of light and Snell's law
Lens formula and power of a lens (P = 1/f)
chapters.exam_tip: Highest marks chapter in Physics. Ray diagram numericals asked every year. Mirror and lens formula must be memorized. Sign convention is critical.

chapters.title: Human Eye and the Colourful World
topics: Structure and working of the human eye
Defects of vision: myopia, hypermetropia, presbyopia
Correction of eye defects using lenses
Atmospheric refraction (twinkling of stars, advance sunrise)
Scattering of light — Tyndall effect, blue sky, red sunset
chapters.exam_tip: Eye defect diagrams and their corrections are asked every year. Reason for blue sky and red sunset must be explained clearly.

chapters.title: Electricity
topics: Heating effect of electric current — Joule's Law
Electric power: P = VI = I²R = V²/R
chapters.exam_tip: Ohm's Law numericals, series/parallel resistance problems are very common. Power formula P=VI=I²R=V²/R must be memorized. Electric energy billing problems also appear.

chapters.title: Magnetic Effects of Electric Current
topics: Magnetic field due to current-carrying straight wire
Magnetic field due to circular loop
Force on current-carrying conductor in magnetic field (Fleming's Left Hand Rule)
Electric motor — working principle and diagram
Electric generator (AC and DC) — working and diagram
Domestic electric circuits — earthing, fuse, MCB
chapters.exam_tip: Electric motor and generator diagrams are asked every year. Fleming rules (Left for motor, Right for generator) must be crystal clear.

chapters.title: Sources of Energy
topics: Fossil fuels — coal, petroleum, natural gas
Solar energy — solar cells and solar panels
Nuclear fission and fusion — nuclear energy
chapters.exam_tip: Lowest marks Physics chapter. Advantages and disadvantages of renewable vs non-renewable sources sufficient. Nuclear energy concepts may appear in MCQ.

chapters.title: Chemical Reactions and Equations
topics: Types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement
Effects of oxidation in everyday life — corrosion, rancidity
chapters.exam_tip: Balancing chemical equations is very common. ALL 5 types of reactions must be memorized with one example each. Distinguish between oxidation and reduction clearly.

chapters.title: Acids, Bases and Salts
topics: Chemical properties of acids and bases
Common salts: sodium chloride (NaCl), sodium hydroxide (NaOH), baking soda (NaHCO₃), washing soda (Na₂CO₃·10H₂O), bleaching powder (CaOCl₂), plaster of paris (CaSO₄·½H₂O)
chapters.exam_tip: Chemical formulas and uses of common salts are very frequently asked. pH of common substances. Difference between acids and bases based on indicators.

chapters.title: Metals and Non-metals
topics: Physical properties of metals and non-metals
Chemical properties of metals (reactions with oxygen, water, acids, other metal salts)
Extraction of metals from ores (least reactive, moderately reactive, highly reactive)
chapters.exam_tip: Reactivity series order must be memorized (top to bottom: K, Na, Ca, Mg, Al, Zn, Fe, Pb, H, Cu, Hg, Ag, Au, Pt). Extraction of aluminium and iron are frequently asked.

chapters.title: Carbon and its Compounds
topics: Allotropes of carbon: diamond, graphite, fullerene
Saturated hydrocarbons (alkanes) and unsaturated hydrocarbons (alkenes, alkynes)
Chemical properties of carbon compounds: combustion, oxidation, addition, substitution
Ethanoic acid (acetic acid): properties and uses
Soaps and detergents: structure, micelle formation, difference
chapters.exam_tip: IUPAC naming of carbon compounds is frequently asked. Difference between soaps and detergents (micelle, hard water behavior) is very common in short answers.

chapters.title: Periodic Classification of Elements
topics: Early attempts at classification: Dobereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves
Mendeleev's Periodic Table — merits and limitations
Modern Periodic Table — periods and groups
Trends in modern periodic table: atomic size, metallic/non-metallic character, valency
chapters.exam_tip: Lowest marks Chemistry chapter. Compare Mendeleev vs Modern periodic table. Position of hydrogen controversy. Trends in periodic table.

chapters.title: Life Processes
topics: Nutrition: autotrophic (photosynthesis — equation, conditions, stomata) and heterotrophic (holozoic, parasitic, saprophytic, symbiotic)
Nutrition in humans — alimentary canal, digestive glands
Respiration: aerobic and anaerobic, cellular respiration, ATP
Transportation in plants: xylem (water), phloem (food), transpiration
Transportation in humans: heart structure, blood vessels, blood composition, lymph
Excretion in humans: kidneys, nephron, dialysis
chapters.exam_tip: Highest marks Biology chapter. Photosynthesis equation, heart diagram, nephron diagram are asked every year. Know all 4 life processes in depth.

chapters.title: Control and Coordination
topics: Nervous system: neurons, types of neurons, synapse
Human brain: cerebrum, cerebellum, medulla oblongata
Reflex action and reflex arc — diagram
Plant hormones: auxin, gibberellin, cytokinin, abscisic acid
Tropic movements in plants: phototropism, geotropism, hydrotropism, chemotropism
Human endocrine system: pituitary, thyroid, adrenal, pancreas, gonads
Hormones: insulin, adrenalin, thyroxine, growth hormone
Difference between nervous and hormonal control
chapters.exam_tip: Reflex arc diagram and difference between nervous vs hormonal control are very frequently asked. Know all plant hormones and their functions.

chapters.title: How do Organisms Reproduce?
topics: Asexual reproduction: fission (binary and multiple), budding, spore formation, vegetative propagation, fragmentation, regeneration
Sexual reproduction in flowering plants: flower structure, pollination, fertilization, fruits and seeds
Reproduction in humans: male reproductive system, female reproductive system
chapters.exam_tip: Human reproductive system diagrams come frequently. Difference between sexual and asexual reproduction. Pollination types.

chapters.title: Heredity and Evolution
topics: Mendel's experiments: monohybrid cross (3:1), dihybrid cross (9:3:3:1)
Sex determination in humans (XX and XY)
Charles Darwin's theory of natural selection
chapters.exam_tip: Monohybrid cross problems (Punnett square) may appear in short or long answer. Sex determination mechanism (XX/XY) is frequently asked.

chapters.title: Our Environment
topics: Trophic levels and energy flow (10% law)
chapters.exam_tip: Definitions of ecosystem, food chain, trophic levels. Biodegradable vs non-biodegradable examples. Ozone depletion causes (CFCs).

chapters.title: Sustainable Management of Natural Resources
topics: Natural resources: forests, wildlife, water, coal, petroleum
Water conservation methods: dams, watershed management, rainwater harvesting
chapters.exam_tip: Lowest marks Biology chapter. Chipko movement and Khejri tree protection (Bishnoi community). Advantages and disadvantages of dams. 3 Rs definition.

social_science.note: Social Science is currently outside Zuno's teaching scope. Exam pattern info only.

hindi.note: Hindi is currently outside Zuno's teaching scope. Exam pattern info only.

english.note: English is currently outside Zuno's teaching scope. English marks excluded from division calculation.

sanskrit_urdu.note: Sanskrit/Urdu is currently outside Zuno's teaching scope. Exam pattern info only.
